evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Deliver projects safely, on time, and cost effectively while upholding IT governance.
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Repeated governance, quality, lineage, and reliability language throughout ('ensuring quality, lineage, and reliability', 'establish monitoring, alerting, and lifecycle management', 'upholding IT governance') dominates with no velocity/shipping-speed phrases present.
evidence.domain_risk: Analyze multi-source data to optimize strategies, operations, and portfolio decisions.
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: "Portfolio decisions" combined with IT governance and safe/compliant delivery framing signals a finance/investment-adjacent, high-consequence context.
evidence.data_team_maturity: within data lake and data mesh frameworks
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Data mesh and data lake frameworks are named as existing infrastructure the candidate operates within, signalling an established, specialised data function rather than one being built from scratch.
evidence.jd_authorship: SQL Server Integration Services (SSIS) experience for ETL workflows. Data visualization and reporting technologies, particularly AWS QuickSight (preferable), MS Fabric/PBI or QlikSense.
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Requirements name precise tools (SSIS, QuickSight, MS Fabric/PBI, QlikSense, AWS) with application context, but responsibilities lean on generic consultancy phrasing ('propose process optimizations', 'demonstrate resilience through organizational change'), producing a mixed signal typical of staffing-agency JDs.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Support teams with datasets for building, standardizing, and maintaining reporting and self-service analytics within data lake and data mesh frameworks.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role serves internal data consumers and platform teams through self-service analytics infrastructure rather than a specific commercial, product, or finance function.
evidence.autonomy_level: Strong autonomy to lead analytic solutions from design to deployment.
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The requirement explicitly names end-to-end ownership from design through deployment, a strategic framing.
evidence.ai_role: support metrics, analytics, AI/machine learning, and self-service business intelligence
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The candidate builds data architecture and feature-engineered datasets that support downstream AI/ML systems, making the AI consumer of the candidate's output rather than a tool the candidate uses.
evidence.testing_framing: Preprocess and engineer features from structured and unstructured data, ensuring quality, lineage, and reliability.
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Data quality, lineage, and reliability are framed as something the candidate ensures through their own engineering work, an ownership verb rather than a tool listed in a skills section.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Deliver projects safely, on time, and cost effectively while upholding IT governance.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Governance, quality, lineage, and reliability language recurs across nearly every responsibility bullet, and appears early in the role framing, indicating risk/trust concerns dominate rather than being secondary.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Implement models and data products with cross-functional teams; establish monitoring, alerting, and lifecycle management.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The role both implements new models/data products (greenfield) and establishes monitoring/lifecycle management for existing data lake and mesh infrastructure (fix_scale), producing a mixed signal.
evidence.language_gate: Fluency in English is a requirement; Portuguese proficiency strongly preferred.
evidence.urgency: Posted 15 hours ago. Be among the first 25 applicants.
evidence.loss_aversion: Deliver projects safely, on time, and cost effectively while upholding IT governance — safety and governance framing recurs across the responsibilities, anchoring a high risk register.
